tasks app / collaboration

Threaded comments on task cards

proposed

Implementation plan for adding threaded discussion to task cards, so review feedback and blockers stay attached to the work instead of scattering across chat.

2 wks
estimated effort
3
packages touched
web, api, realtime
2
new tables
comment_threads
feature flag

Milestones

  1. Schema and API contract done

    Migrations for comment_threads and comments merged; REST contract for create/list/resolve reviewed and signed off by the api team.

  2. Composer and thread UI in progress

    Inline composer on the task card, thread list with optimistic posting, behind the comment_threads flag for internal accounts only.

  3. Realtime fanout pending

    Wire new comments into the existing presence channel so open cards update live without a refresh.

  4. Staged rollout pending

    Flag on for one workspace, watch write volume and fanout latency for a week, then expand.

Data flow

The api is the only writer to the comments table — the composer never writes directly, so validation and mention parsing happen in one place. Subscribed clients receive the new comment over the existing presence channel within the same round trip, with no separate polling path to keep in sync.

Schema

migrations/0042_comment_threads.sql
-- one thread per task card; comments belong to a thread
create table comment_threads (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  task_id       uuid not null references tasks(id),
  resolved_at   timestamptz,
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
);

create table comments (
  id            uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  thread_id     uuid not null references comment_threads(id),
  author_id     uuid not null references users(id),
  body          text not null,
  created_at    timestamptz not null default now()
);

create index comments_thread_id_idx on comments(thread_id);

Decisions

Decision Owner Status Rationale
One thread per task, not per field Design Settled Matches how reviewers actually discuss cards — one conversation, not one per checklist item.
Comments are edit-only for 5 minutes Priya N. Settled Matches the existing edit window on task descriptions; avoids a separate policy to maintain.
No nested replies in v1 Priya N. Settled Flat threads cover the reported use cases and skip a recursive UI and query pattern.
Mentions reuse the existing notification pipeline Marcus T. Open Pipeline wasn't built for per-comment granularity; needs a spike before commit eng.

Risks and rollout

Flag on for everyone at once

Simplest to ship and support — one code path, no per-workspace state to track. Any write-volume or fanout problem shows up for every workspace simultaneously.

best for: a change with a well-understood blast radius

Staged by workspace

Recommended. Enable comment_threads for one internal workspace first, watch write volume and fanout latency for a week, then expand in batches.

best for: this feature, given the new realtime fanout path is unproven at scale

Unproven at scale. The realtime fanout hasn't carried comment-level write volume before — presence updates are far less frequent. A busy card with a fast back-and-forth thread could produce more fanout events per second than the channel has seen in production.

Ship in the order above: schema, then composer and thread UI, then realtime fanout, then staged rollout. Each slice is independently useful to verify — the schema and API contract can be reviewed without any UI, and the composer can be demoed behind the flag before realtime fanout is wired in, so a slow spot in any one slice doesn't block review of the others.

Start the staged rollout only after the fanout milestone has run for at least three days without a fanout-latency regression on the internal workspace.

Sources: tasks-app repo (packages/web, packages/api, packages/realtime), migration 0042 draft, and the Jul 2 sync with Priya N. and Marcus T. on rollout sequencing.
API contract sketch

POST /tasks/:taskId/comments — creates the thread on first comment if none exists, returns the created comment. GET /tasks/:taskId/comments — returns the thread with comments ordered by created_at. POST /threads/:threadId/resolve — sets resolved_at; resolved threads remain readable but the composer collapses by default.
